title: Company-specific employee records
user_story: As a Company/HR Admin, I want each employee record to be scoped to a single company, so that workforce data remains isolated and accurate per company.
acceptance_criteria: Given I create an employee, when the record is saved, then it is associated with exactly one company.; Given the same physical person is employed by two companies, when records are created, then a separate employee record exists in each company rather than a shared record.

title: Employee with or without a linked user account
user_story: As a Company/HR Admin, I want to maintain an employee record either standalone or linked to a user account, so that I can track staff who do not need system access as well as those who do.
acceptance_criteria: Given I create an employee, when no system access is required, then the record can be saved without a linked user account.; Given an employee requires system access, when I link a user account, then the employee record is associated with that account.

title: Core organizational assignments
user_story: As a Company/HR Admin, I want to assign each employee to a company, jurisdiction, department, and position, so that their placement in the organization is clearly defined.
acceptance_criteria: Given I create or edit an employee, when I save the record, then exactly one company, one jurisdiction, and one position are assigned.; Given an employee record, when I assign departments, then at least one department is required and multiple departments may be assigned.

title: Group and multi-location assignment
user_story: As a Company/HR Admin, I want to optionally assign employees to groups and to one or more locations, so that I can reflect team membership and where staff operate.
acceptance_criteria: Given an employee record, when I assign groups, then zero or more groups may be linked.; Given an employee who works across sites, when I assign locations, then one or more operating locations may be recorded.

title: Employee lifecycle events
user_story: As a Company/HR Admin, I want employee records to support lifecycle events such as onboarding, probation, performance, transfers, and exit, so that I can manage the full employment journey in one place.
acceptance_criteria: Given an employee record, when a lifecycle event occurs (onboarding, probation, performance, transfer, or exit), then it can be captured against the record.; Given lifecycle events are recorded, when I review an employee, then their current lifecycle stage is visible.

title: Primary reporting manager
user_story: As a Company/HR Admin, I want each employee to have exactly one primary reporting manager, so that the core reporting structure is unambiguous.
acceptance_criteria: Given an employee record, when I set the reporting structure, then exactly one primary manager is assigned.; Given an employee, when no primary manager is set or more than one is attempted, then the system enforces the single primary manager rule.

title: Dotted-line (matrix) managers
user_story: As a Company/HR Admin, I want to optionally assign one or more functional/matrix managers to an employee, so that dotted-line reporting relationships are represented.
acceptance_criteria: Given an employee record, when I add dotted-line managers, then zero, one, or more functional managers may be assigned.; Given dotted-line managers exist, when I view the employee, then they are shown as distinct from the primary manager.

title: Temporary/acting manager with delegation
user_story: As a Manager, I want to assign a temporary/acting manager for my reports, so that responsibilities are delegated automatically while I am unavailable.
acceptance_criteria: Given an employee, when a temporary/acting manager is assigned, then the assignment is recorded against the employee.; Given a temporary manager assignment, when it is active, then delegation to the acting manager occurs automatically.

title: Effective-dated manager changes with audit trail
user_story: As a Company/HR Admin, I want all manager assignment changes to be effective-dated with a complete audit trail, so that reporting history is accurate and traceable.
acceptance_criteria: Given I change a manager assignment, when I save it, then an effective date is required.; Given manager changes have been made, when I review history, then a complete audit trail of each change is available.

title: Duplicate detection on government IDs
user_story: As a Company/HR Admin, I want the system to enforce uniqueness of government identification fields (Aadhar, Passport, PAN) with deduplication prompts, so that duplicate employee records are prevented.
acceptance_criteria: Given I enter an Aadhar, Passport, or PAN, when the value already exists, then the system flags it and prompts for deduplication.; Given a duplicate government ID is detected, when I proceed, then I am guided to resolve the duplicate before saving.

title: Statutory workforce data capture (Indian labor law)
user_story: As a Company/HR Admin, I want to capture statutory workforce data such as UAN, ESIC number, ESI/PF eligibility, Professional Tax registration, LWF applicability, maternity benefit eligibility, and gratuity eligibility, so that the company remains compliant with Indian labor law.
acceptance_criteria: Given an employee record, when I enter statutory details, then UAN, ESIC number, ESI/PF eligibility status, Professional Tax registration, LWF applicability, maternity benefit eligibility, and gratuity eligibility can all be captured.; Given statutory fields are captured, when I view the employee, then these details are available for compliance reporting.

title: Leave balances with statutory entitlements
user_story: As a Company/HR Admin, I want to track employee leave balances with their statutory entitlements, so that leave records reflect legally mandated allowances.
acceptance_criteria: Given an employee record, when leave data is maintained, then leave balances are tracked alongside statutory entitlements.; Given leave balances exist, when I review an employee, then current balances and statutory entitlements are visible.
